addedBlocks:
## pencil
The Pencil MCP server is the editor for reading and writing to .pen files and gives you tools to be an expert design generator and validator specifically for web and mobile applications and websites.

- IMPORTANT: The contents of the .pen files are encrypted and can be only access via the "pencil" MCP tools. DO NOT use "Read" or "Grep" tools to read contents of .pen files.
- IMPORTANT: Make sure to ONLY use the "pencil" MCP tools when reading or searching contents of .pen files (batch_get) and making changes inside .pen files (batch_design).
- IMPORTANT: Always follow the tool definitions and formats for the "pencil" MCP tools.

Available tools:
- "get_editor_state()": Start with this tool if you are aware of the current editor state. Use this tool to determine in the beginning of a task, to understand what is the currently active .pen file you want to work on and the current user selection in it and other essential context information.
- "open_document(filePathOrNew)": When there is no active editor opened, pass 'new' to create an empty .pen file or if the user explicitly ask to open a specific .pen file use its path to open it.
- "get_guidelines(topic=code|table|tailwind|landing-page|slides|design-system|mobile-app|web-app)": This tool returns design guidelines and rules for working with .pen files. Only use it if you need one of the available topics for your design task. ONLY use the topics defined in the tool's description.
- "get_style_guide_tags": Use this tool after using "get_guidelines" for additional design inspiration with "get_style_guide".
- "get_style_guide(tags, name)": This tool returns a style guide to be used in the ongoing design task based on a set of relevant tags or a specific style guide by name. Ask for a style guide when designing screens, websites, apps or dashboards and not working with a design system already.
- "batch_get(patterns, nodeIds)": Retrieve nodes by searching for matching patterns or by reading node ids in batches. Use this for discovering and understand .pen files.
- "batc… [truncated]

content.thinking: The user wants to make the Nuxt dev server accessible on the intranet (not just localhost). Let me check the current configuration.

content.content:
1	import { readFileSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs'
2	import { execSync } from 'node:child_process'
3	import { resolve } from 'node:path'
4	
5	declare const process: { env: Record<string, string | undefined> }
6	const apiBase = process.env.NUXT_API_BASE || 'http://localhost:8000'
7	const minioBase = process.env.NUXT_MINIO_BASE || 'http://121.31.38.35:19000/devtrack-uploads'
8	
9	function getBuildInfo() {
10	  const versionFile = resolve(__dirname, 'VERSION')
11	  if (existsSync(versionFile)) {
12	    const content = readFileSync(versionFile, 'utf-8').trim()
13	    const parts = content.split('-')
14	    if (parts.length >= 4) {
15	      const gitHash = parts[parts.length - 1]
16	      const buildDate = parts.slice(0, -1).join('-')
17	      return { gitHash, buildDate }
18	    }
19	  }
20	  try {
21	    const gitHash = execSync('git rev-parse --short HEAD', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim()
22	    return { gitHash, buildDate: null }
23	  } catch {
24	    return { gitHash: null, buildDate: null }
25	  }
26	}
27	
28	const buildInfo = getBuildInfo()
29	const pkg = JSON.parse(readFileSync(resolve(__dirname, 'package.json'), 'utf-8'))
30	const nuxtVersion = pkg.dependencies?.nuxt?.replace(/^\^/, '') || ''
31	const vueVersion = pkg.dependencies?.vue?.replace(/^\^/, '') || ''
32	
33	export default defineNuxtConfig({
34	  ssr: false,
35	  devtools: { enabled: false },
36	  devServer: { port: 3000 },
37	  modules: ['@nuxt/ui'],
38	  css: ['~/assets/css/main.css'],
39	  colorMode: { preference: 'light', fallback: 'light' },
40	  runtimeConfig: {
41	    public: {
42	      gitHash: buildInfo.gitHash || '',
43	      buildDate: buildInfo.buildDate || '',
44	      nuxtVersion,
45	      vueVersion,
46	    },
47	  },
48	  app: {
49	    baseURL: '/',
50	    head: {
51	      title: 'DevTrakr - 项目管理平台',
52	      meta: [
53	        { name: 'viewport', content: 'width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no, viewport-fit=cover' },
54	      ],
55	      link: [
56	        { rel: 'icon', type: 'image/svg+xml', href: '/favicon.svg' },
57	      ],
58	    },
59	  },
60	  routeRules: {
61	    '/api/**': { proxy: `${apiBase}/api/**` },
62	    '/uploads/**': { proxy: `${minioBase}/**` },
63	  },
64	  nitro: {
65	    devProxy: {
66	      '/api/': {
67	        target: `${apiBase}/api/`,
68	        changeOrigin: true,
69	      },
70	      '/uploads/': {
71	        target: `${minioBase}/`,
72	        changeOrigin: true,
73	      },
74	    },
75	  },
76	  compatibilityDate: '2025-01-01',
77	})

content.text: Two changes needed: bind the dev server to `0.0.0.0` and update the launch config to match.

content.content: The file /Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/frontend/nuxt.config.ts has been updated successfully.
The file /Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/.vscode/launch.json has been updated successfully.

content.text:
Done. Two changes:

1. **[nuxt.config.ts:36](frontend/nuxt.config.ts#L36)** — Added `host: '0.0.0.0'` to `devServer` so Nuxt listens on all network interfaces instead of just localhost.
2. **[launch.json:22](.vscode/launch.json#L22)** — Updated the `serverReadyAction` pattern to match the `0.0.0.0` address Nuxt will now print.

The dev server will now be accessible at `http://<your-ip>:3000` from other devices on the intranet.
<ide_opened_file>The user opened the file /Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/backend/apps/notifications/urls.py in the IDE. This may or may not be related to the current task.</ide_opened_file>
我发现改了这两个文件,本地启动服务页面要好久才能打开

content.thinking:
The user is saying that after the changes, the local dev server takes a long time to open the page. This is likely because binding to `0.0.0.0` can cause DNS resolution delays in the browser when it tries to connect. Let me revert the approach - instead of binding to all interfaces in the config permanently, a better approach might be to only use `0.0.0.0` in the launch config args.

Actually, the real issue is likely that with `host: '0.0.0.0'`, Nuxt's dev server output changes from `http://localhost:3000` to `http://0.0.0.0:3000`, and the browser may have trouble resolving `0.0.0.0`. 

The serverReadyAction should still open localhost, but the slowness suggests I need to move the host binding out of the permanent config. I'll revert the nuxt.config change and instead pass `--host 0.0.0.0` only through the launch.json args so it only applies when launching from VSCode. I'll revert the nuxt.config to use localhost and instead pass the `--host` flag through the VSCode launch configuration to enable intranet access only when debugging.

content.text: 这是 `0.0.0.0` 绑定导致的 DNS 解析延迟问题。改为只在 VSCode launch 配置中传 `--host` 参数，不改 nuxt.config，这样普通启动不受影响。

content.content: The file /Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/frontend/nuxt.config.ts has been updated successfully.
The file /Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/.vscode/launch.json has been updated successfully.

content.text:
改回来了。现在：

- **`nuxt.config.ts`** — 保持默认 `port: 3000`，不设 host
- **[launch.json:18](.vscode/launch.json#L18)** — 通过 `--host 0.0.0.0` 参数启动，仅在 VSCode 调试时监听所有网卡

这样 `npm run dev` 还是走 localhost（快），VSCode 启动时才开放内网访问。

写一个宪法,必须要我同意或者我明确指出,才可以 deploy to production (env/prod)

content.thinking: The user wants me to save a rule that deploying to production (env/prod) requires their explicit approval. This is a feedback memory.

content.content: File does not exist. Note: your current working directory is /Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack.

toolUseResult: Error: File does not exist. Note: your current working directory is /Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack.

input.content:
---
name: Never deploy to production without explicit approval
description: MUST get user's explicit consent before pushing to env/prod — constitutional rule, no exceptions
type: feedback
---

Never push to `env/prod` unless the user explicitly says to deploy to production.

**Why:** User established this as a constitutional rule — production deploys carry high risk and must always be a deliberate, user-initiated action.

**How to apply:** Even if the user says "deploy" without specifying environment, do NOT assume production. Always confirm "env/prod" specifically. Only `git push origin main:env/prod` (or equivalent) after the user explicitly approves or requests it. Pushing to `env/test` is acceptable when contextually appropriate, but `env/prod` is always gated on explicit consent.

input.content: - [No prod deploy without approval](feedback_no_prod_deploy.md) — CONSTITUTIONAL: never push env/prod without explicit user consent

toolUseResult.content: - [No prod deploy without approval](feedback_no_prod_deploy.md) — CONSTITUTIONAL: never push env/prod without explicit user consent

content.text:
已写入宪法。从现在起：

- **`env/prod`** — 必须你明确同意或主动要求，我才会执行
- **`env/test`** — 上下文合理时可以正常推送
- 即使你说"部署"但没指定环境，我也不会假设是 production，会先确认
